1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class UpdateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::InternalEventsTracking
6:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
7: 
8:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
9:       super
10: 
11:       @target_branch_was_deleted = @params.delete(:target_branch_was_deleted)
12:     end
13: 
14:     def execute(merge_request)
15:       if Gitlab::Utils.to_boolean(params[:draft])
16:         merge_request.title = merge_request.draft_title
17:       end
18: 
19:       if params.key?(:merge_after)
20:         merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
21:         UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
22:       end
23: 
24:       update_merge_request_with_specialized_service(merge_request) || general_fallback(merge_request)
25:     end
26: 
27:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
28:       super
29:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
30:       old_labels = old_associations.fetch(:labels, [])
31:       old_mentioned_users = old_associations.fetch(:mentioned_users, [])
32:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
33:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
34:       old_timelogs = old_associations.fetch(:timelogs, [])
35:       changed_fields = merge_request.previous_changes.keys
36: 
37:       resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
38: 
39:       if merge_request.previous_changes.include?('title') ||
40:           merge_request.previous_changes.include?('description')
41:         todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_mentioned_users)
42:         handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, merge_request.previous_changes.keys)
43:       end
44: 
45:       handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
46:       handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
47: 
48:       track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
49:       track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
50:       track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
51:       track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
52: 
53:       notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
54:       notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
55: 
56:       # Since #mark_as_unchecked triggers an update action through the MR's
57:       #   state machine, we want to push this as far down in the process so we
58:       #   avoid resetting #ActiveModel::Dirty
59:       #
60:       if changed_fields.include?('target_branch') ||
61:           changed_fields.include?('source_branch')
62:         merge_request.mark_as_unchecked unless merge_request.unchecked?
63: 
64:         # Delete HEAD diff and re-enqueue mergeability check so we get to generate
65:         # a fresh HEAD diff when either branches have been changed.
66:         merge_request.merge_head_diff&.destroy!
67:         merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
68:       end
69:     end
70: 
71:     def handle_task_changes(merge_request)
72:       todo_service.resolve_todos_for_target(merge_request, current_user)
73:       todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def after_update(merge_request, old_associations)
77:       super
78: 
79:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
80:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
81:     end
82: 
83:     private
84: 
85:     attr_reader :target_branch_was_deleted
86: 
87:     def trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(merge_request, old_closing_issues_ids)
88:       new_issue_ids = merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
89:       all_issue_ids = new_issue_ids | old_closing_issues_ids
90:       return if all_issue_ids.blank?
91: 
92:       WorkItem.id_in(all_issue_ids).find_each(batch_size: 100) do |work_item| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
93:         GraphqlTriggers.work_item_updated(work_item)
94:       end
95:     end
96: 
97:     override :associations_before_update
98:     def associations_before_update(merge_request)
99:       super.merge(
100:         closing_issues_ids: merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
101:       )
102:     end
103: 
104:     override :change_state
105:     def change_state(merge_request, state_event)
106:       return unless super
107: 
108:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
109:     end
110: 
111:     override :trigger_update_subscriptions
112:     def trigger_update_subscriptions(merge_request, old_associations)
113:       return unless @trigger_work_item_updated
114: 
115:       trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(
116:         merge_request,
117:         old_associations.fetch(:closing_issues_ids, [])
118:       )
119:     end
120: 
121:     def general_fallback(merge_request)
122:       # We don't allow change of source/target projects and source branch
123:       # after merge request was created
124:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
125:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
126:       params.delete(:source_branch)
127: 
128:       if merge_request.closed_or_merged_without_fork?
129:         params.delete(:target_branch)
130:         params.delete(:force_remove_source_branch)
131:       end
132: 
133:       update_task_event(merge_request) || update(merge_request)
134:     end
135: 
136:     def handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, changed_fields)
137:       fields = %w[title description]
138: 
139:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| fields.include?(field) }
140:       return unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
141:       return unless should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
142: 
143:       ::Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
144:         ::MergeRequests::AutoMerge::TitleDescriptionUpdateEvent.new(data: { current_user_id: current_user.id, merge_request_id: merge_request.id })
145:       )
146:     end
147: 
148:     def should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
149:       changed_fields.include?('title') &&
150:         merge_request.project.merge_request_title_regex.present?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
154:       tracked_fields = %w[title description]
155: 
156:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| tracked_fields.include?(field) }
157: 
158:       tracked_fields.each do |action|
159:         next unless changed_fields.include?(action)
160: 
161:         merge_request_activity_counter
162:           .public_send(:"track_#{action}_edit_action", user: current_user) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
163:       end
164:     end
165: 
166:     def track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
167:       return unless changed_fields.include?('discussion_locked')
168: 
169:       if merge_request.discussion_locked
170:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_locked_action(user: current_user)
171:       else
172:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_unlocked_action(user: current_user)
173:       end
174:     end
175: 
176:     def track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
177:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_estimate_changed_action(user: current_user) if changed_fields.include?('time_estimate')
178:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_spent_changed_action(user: current_user) if old_timelogs != merge_request.timelogs
179:     end
180: 
181:     def track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
182:       return if Set.new(merge_request.labels) == Set.new(old_labels)
183: 
184:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_labels_changed_action(user: current_user)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
188:       added_labels = merge_request.labels - old_labels
189: 
190:       return unless added_labels.present?
191: 
192:       notification_service.async.relabeled_merge_request(
193:         merge_request,
194:         added_labels,
195:         current_user
196:       )
197:     end
198: 
199:     def notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
200:       added_mentions = merge_request.mentioned_users(current_user) - old_mentioned_users
201: 
202:       return unless added_mentions.present?
203: 
204:       notification_service.async.new_mentions_in_merge_request(
205:         merge_request,
206:         added_mentions,
207:         current_user
208:       )
209:     end
210: 
211:     def resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
212:       return unless has_changes?(merge_request, old_labels: old_labels, old_assignees: old_assignees, old_reviewers: old_reviewers)
213: 
214:       resolve_todos_for(merge_request)
215:     end
216: 
217:     def handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changes)
218:       return unless changes.include?('target_branch')
219: 
220:       create_branch_change_note(
221:         merge_request,
222:         'target',
223:         target_branch_was_deleted ? 'delete' : 'update',
224:         merge_request.previous_changes['target_branch'].first,
225:         merge_request.target_branch
226:       )
227: 
228:       delete_approvals_on_target_branch_change(merge_request)
229: 
230:       # `target_branch_was_deleted` is set to true when MR gets re-targeted due to
231:       # deleted target branch. In this case we don't want to create a new pipeline
232:       # on behalf of MR author.
233:       # We nullify head_pipeline_id to force that a new pipeline is explicitly
234:       # created in order to pass mergeability checks.
235:       if target_branch_was_deleted
236:         merge_request.head_pipeline_id = nil
237:         merge_request.retargeted = true
238: 
239:         track_internal_event(
240:           'retarget_merge_request_on_target_branch_merge',
241:           user: current_user,
242:           project: merge_request.target_project
243:         )
244:       else
245:         refresh_pipelines_on_merge_requests(merge_request, allow_duplicate: true)
246:       end
247: 
248:       abort_auto_merge(merge_request, 'target branch was changed')
249:     end
250: 
251:     def handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
252:       return unless changed_fields.include?("title")
253: 
254:       old_title, new_title = merge_request.previous_changes["title"]
255:       old_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(old_title)
256:       new_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(new_title)
257: 
258:       if old_title_draft != new_title_draft
259:         # notify the draft status changed. Added/removed message is handled in the
260:         # email template itself, see `change_in_merge_request_draft_status_email` template.
261:         notify_draft_status_changed(merge_request)
262:         trigger_merge_request_status_updated(merge_request)
263:         publish_draft_change_event(merge_request, new_draft_status: new_title_draft)
264:       end
265: 
266:       if !old_title_draft && new_title_draft
267:         # Marked as Draft
268:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_marked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
269:       elsif old_title_draft && !new_title_draft
270:         # Unmarked as Draft
271:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_unmarked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
272:       end
273:     end
274: 
275:     def publish_draft_change_event(merge_request, new_draft_status:)
276:       Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
277:         MergeRequests::DraftStateChangeEvent.new(
278:           data: {
279:             current_user_id: current_user.id,
280:             merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
281:             new_draft_status: new_draft_status
282:           }
283:         )
284:       )
285:     end
286: 
287:     def notify_draft_status_changed(merge_request)
288:       notification_service.async.change_in_merge_request_draft_status(
289:         merge_request,
290:         current_user
291:       )
292:     end
293: 
294:     def create_branch_change_note(issuable, branch_type, event_type, old_branch, new_branch)
295:       SystemNoteService.change_branch(
296:         issuable, issuable.project, current_user, branch_type, event_type,
297:         old_branch, new_branch)
298:     end
299: 
300:     override :before_update
301:     def before_update(merge_request, skip_spam_check: false)
302:       merge_request.check_for_spam(user: current_user, action: :update) unless skip_spam_check
303:     end
304: 
305:     override :handle_quick_actions
306:     def handle_quick_actions(merge_request)
307:       super
308: 
309:       # Ensure this parameter does not get used as an attribute
310:       rebase = params.delete(:rebase)
311: 
312:       if rebase
313:         rebase_from_quick_action(merge_request)
314:         # Ignore "/merge" if "/rebase" is used to avoid an unexpected race
315:         params.delete(:merge)
316:       end
317: 
318:       merge_from_quick_action(merge_request) if params[:merge]
319:     end
320: 
321:     def rebase_from_quick_action(merge_request)
322:       merge_request.rebase_async(current_user.id)
323:     end
324: 
325:     def merge_from_quick_action(merge_request)
326:       last_diff_sha = params.delete(:merge)
327: 
328:       ::MergeRequests::MergeOrchestrationService
329:         .new(project, current_user, { sha: last_diff_sha })
330:         .execute(merge_request)
331:     end
332: 
333:     override :quick_action_options
334:     def quick_action_options
335:       {
336:         merge_request_diff_head_sha: params.delete(:merge_request_diff_head_sha),
337:         scope_validator: params[:scope_validator]
338:       }
339:     end
340: 
341:     def update_merge_request_with_specialized_service(merge_request)
342:       return unless params.delete(:use_specialized_service)
343: 
344:       # If we're attempting to modify only a single attribute, look up whether
345:       #   we have a specialized, targeted service we should use instead. We may
346:       #   in the future extend this to include specialized services that operate
347:       #   on multiple attributes, but for now limit to only single attribute
348:       #   updates.
349:       #
350:       return unless params.one?
351: 
352:       attempt_specialized_update_services(merge_request, params.each_key.first.to_sym)
353:     end
354: 
355:     def attempt_specialized_update_services(merge_request, attribute)
356:       case attribute
357:       when :assignee_ids, :assignee_id
358:         assignees_service.execute(merge_request)
359:       when :spend_time
360:         add_time_spent_service.execute(merge_request)
361:       end
362:     end
363: 
364:     def assignees_service
365:       @assignees_service ||= ::MergeRequests::UpdateAssigneesService
366:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
367:     end
368: 
369:     def add_time_spent_service
370:       @add_time_spent_service ||= ::MergeRequests::AddSpentTimeService.new(project: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
371:     end
372: 
373:     def new_user_ids(merge_request, user_ids, attribute)
374:       # prime the cache - prevent N+1 lookup during authorization loop.
375:       return [] if user_ids.empty?
376: 
377:       merge_request.project.team.max_member_access_for_user_ids(user_ids)
378:       User.id_in(user_ids).map do |user|
379:         link_composite_identity(user) if user.composite_identity_enforced? && user.service_account?
380: 
381:         if user.can?(:read_merge_request, merge_request)
382:           user.id
383:         else
384:           merge_request.errors.add(
385:             attribute,
386:             "Cannot assign #{user.to_reference} to #{merge_request.to_reference}"
387:           )
388:           nil
389:         end
390:       end.compact
391:     end
392: 
393:     def link_composite_identity(user)
394:       ::Gitlab::Auth::Identity.link_from_scoped_user(user, current_user)
395:     end
396: 
397:     def resolve_todos_for(merge_request)
398:       service_user = current_user
399: 
400:       merge_request.run_after_commit_or_now do
401:         ::MergeRequests::ResolveTodosService.new(merge_request, service_user).async_execute

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class AfterCreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute(merge_request)
6:       merge_request.ensure_merge_request_diff
7: 
8:       prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
9:       prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
10:       mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
11: 
12:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executing hooks'))
13:       execute_hooks(merge_request)
14:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executed hooks'))
15:     end
16: 
17:     private
18: 
19:     def prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
20:       if async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
21:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline async'))
22:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true)
23:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline creating async'))
24:       else
25:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline'))
26:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: false)
27:         merge_request.update_head_pipeline
28:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline created'))
29:       end
30: 
31:       check_mergeability(merge_request)
32:     end
33: 
34:     def prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
35:       event_service.open_mr(merge_request, current_user)
36: 
37:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_create_mr_action(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
38:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
39: 
40:       notification_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
41:       merge_request.diffs(include_stats: false).write_cache
42:       merge_request.create_cross_references!(current_user)
43:       todo_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
44:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user)
45: 
46:       Gitlab::InternalEvents.track_event(
47:         'create_merge_request',
48:         user: current_user,
49:         project: merge_request.target_project
50:       )
51: 
52:       link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
56:       LinkLfsObjectsService.new(project: merge_request.target_project).execute(merge_request)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def check_mergeability(merge_request)
60:       return unless merge_request.preparing?
61: 
62:       # Need to set to unchecked to be able to check for mergeability or else
63:       # it'll be a no-op.
64:       merge_request.mark_as_unchecked
65:       merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
66:     end
67: 
68:     def mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
69:       merge_request.update!(prepared_at: Time.current)
70:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
71:     end
72: 
73:     def logger
74:       @logger ||= Gitlab::AppLogger
75:     end
76: 
77:     def log_payload(merge_request, message)
78:       Gitlab::ApplicationContext.current.merge(
79:         merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
80:         message: message
81:       )
82:     end
83: 
84:     def async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
85:       Feature.enabled?(:async_mr_pipeline_creation, merge_request.target_project)
86:     end
87:   end
88: end

3: module MergeRequests
4:   class BaseService < ::IssuableBaseService
5:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
6:     include MergeRequests::AssignsMergeParams
7:     include MergeRequests::ErrorLogger
8:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
9: 
10:     delegate :repository, to: :project
11: 
12:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
13:       super(container: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
14:     end
15: 
16:     def create_note(merge_request, state = merge_request.state)
17:       SystemNoteService.change_status(merge_request, merge_request.target_project, current_user, state, nil)
18:     end
19: 
20:     def hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
21:       # NOTE: actioned_at represents when the webhook action occurred.
22:       #   Setting it to Time.current to capture when the webhook was fired for now, but we can consider allowing each service
23:       #   to provide relevant timestamps to override this later if more precision is required (e.g., approved_at, merged_at, closed_at).
24:       actioned_at = Time.current
25: 
26:       hook_data = merge_request.to_hook_data(current_user, old_associations: old_associations, action: action, actioned_at: actioned_at)
27: 
28:       if old_rev && !Gitlab::Git.blank_ref?(old_rev)
29:         hook_data[:object_attributes][:oldrev] = old_rev
30:       end
31: 
32:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system] = system
33:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system_action] = system_action if system && system_action
34: 
35:       hook_data
36:     end
37: 
38:     def execute_hooks(merge_request, action = 'open', old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
39:       # NOTE: Due to the async merge request diffs generation, we need to skip this for CreateService and execute it in
40:       #   AfterCreateService instead so that the webhook consumers receive the update when diffs are ready.
41:       return if merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
42: 
43:       merge_data = Gitlab::Lazy.new { hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: old_rev, old_associations: old_associations, system: system, system_action: system_action) }
44:       merge_request.project.execute_hooks(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
45:       merge_request.project.execute_integrations(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
46: 
47:       execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
48:       execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data) if action == 'open'
49: 
50:       enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
51:     end
52: 
53:     def execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
54:       # Implemented in EE
55:     end
56: 
57:     def execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
58:       return unless merge_request.instance_of?(MergeRequest)
59: 
60:       args = {
61:         'mentionable_type' => 'MergeRequest',
62:         'mentionable_id' => merge_request.id,
63:         'hook_data' => merge_data,
64:         'is_confidential' => false
65:       }
66: 
67:       merge_request.run_after_commit_or_now do
68:         Integrations::GroupMentionWorker.perform_async(args)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
73:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
74:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
75:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
76: 
77:       handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees) if merge_request.assignees != old_assignees
78:       handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers) if merge_request.reviewers != old_reviewers
79:     end
80: 
81:     def handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees)
82:       MergeRequests::HandleAssigneesChangeService
83:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
84:         .async_execute(merge_request, old_assignees)
85:     end
86: 
87:     def handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers)
88:       affected_reviewers = (old_reviewers + merge_request.reviewers) - (old_reviewers & merge_request.reviewers)
89:       create_reviewer_note(merge_request, old_reviewers)
90:       notification_service.async.changed_reviewer_of_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
91:       todo_service.reassigned_reviewable(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
92:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: affected_reviewers.compact)
93:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: merge_request.assignees)
94: 
95:       new_reviewers = merge_request.reviewers - old_reviewers
96:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_users_review_requested(users: new_reviewers)
97:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_reviewers_changed_action(user: current_user)
98:       trigger_merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
99: 
100:       set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
101:       set_first_reviewer_assigned_at_metrics(merge_request) if new_reviewers.any?
102:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
103:       publish_assigned_reviewers_event(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
104:     end
105: 
106:     def cleanup_environments(merge_request)
107:       Environments::StopService.new(merge_request.source_project, current_user)
108:                                .execute_for_merge_request_pipeline(merge_request)
109:     end

111:     def cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
112:       environments = merge_request.environments_in_head_pipeline.in_review_folder.available
113:       environments.each { |environment| environment.cancel_deployment_jobs! }
114:     end

176:     def handle_quick_actions(merge_request)
177:       super
178:       handle_draft_event(merge_request)
179:     end

181:     def handle_draft_event(merge_request)
182:       if draft_event = params.delete(:wip_event)
183:         # We update the title that is provided in the params or we use the mr title
184:         title = params[:title] || merge_request.title
185:         params[:title] = case draft_event
186:                          when 'draft' then MergeRequest.draft_title(title)
187:                          when 'ready' then MergeRequest.draftless_title(title)
188:                          end
189:       end

193:       # Overridden in EE

197:       super
198: 
199:       unless merge_request.can_allow_collaboration?(current_user)
200:         params.delete(:allow_collaboration)
201:       end
202: 
203:       filter_reviewer(merge_request)
204:     end

206:     def filter_reviewer(merge_request)
207:       return if params[:reviewer_ids].blank?
208: 
209:       unless can_admin_issuable?(merge_request)
210:         params.delete(:reviewer_ids)
211: 
212:         return
213:       end
214: 
215:       unless merge_request.allows_multiple_reviewers?
216:         params[:reviewer_ids] = params[:reviewer_ids].first(1)
217:       end
218: 
219:       reviewer_ids = User.id_in(params[:reviewer_ids]).select do |reviewer|
220:         link_composite_identity(reviewer) if reviewer.composite_identity_enforced? && reviewer.service_account?
221: 
222:         user_can_read?(merge_request, reviewer)
223:       end.map(&:id)
224: 
225:       if params[:reviewer_ids].map(&:to_s) == [IssuableFinder::Params::NONE]
226:         params[:reviewer_ids] = []
227:       elsif reviewer_ids.any?

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class NewMergeRequestWorker
4:   include ApplicationWorker
5:   include NewIssuable
6: 
7:   data_consistency :always
8:   sidekiq_options retry: 3
9: 
10:   idempotent!
11:   deduplicate :until_executed
12: 
13:   feature_category :code_review_workflow
14:   urgency :high
15: 
16:   worker_resource_boundary :cpu
17:   weight 2
18: 
19:   def perform(merge_request_id, user_id)
20:     context = { merge_request_id: merge_request_id, user_id: user_id }
21:     xp = Labkit::UserExperienceSli.resume(:create_merge_request, **context)
22: 
23:     Gitlab::QueryLimiting.disable!('https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/337182')
24: 
25:     xp.error!('merge request not ready') unless objects_found?(merge_request_id, user_id)
26:     xp.error!('merge request is already prepared') if issuable&.prepared?
27:     return xp.complete(**context) if xp.has_error?
28: 
29:     MergeRequests::AfterCreateService
30:       .new(project: issuable.target_project, current_user: user)
31:       .execute(issuable)
32: 
33:     xp.complete(**context)
34:   end
35: 
36:   def issuable_class
37:     MergeRequest
38:   end
39: end
40: 
41: NewMergeRequestWorker.prepend_mod

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestCleanupRefsWorker
4:   include ApplicationWorker
5:   include CronjobChildWorker
6:   include LimitedCapacity::Worker
7:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
8: 
9:   data_consistency :always
10: 
11:   sidekiq_options retry: 3
12: 
13:   feature_category :code_review_workflow
14:   idempotent!
15: 
16:   # Hard-coded to 4 for now. Will be configurable later on via application settings.
17:   # This means, there can only be 4 jobs running at the same time at maximum.
18:   MAX_RUNNING_JOBS = 4
19:   FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 3
20: 
21:   def perform_work
22:     unless merge_request
23:       logger.info('No existing merge request to be cleaned up.')
24:       return
25:     end
26: 
27:     log_extra_metadata_on_done(:merge_request_id, merge_request.id)
28: 
29:     result = MergeRequests::CleanupRefsService.new(merge_request).execute
30: 
31:     if result[:status] == :success
32:       merge_request_cleanup_schedule.complete!
33:     else
34:       if merge_request_cleanup_schedule.failed_count < FAILURE_THRESHOLD
35:         merge_request_cleanup_schedule.retry!
36:       else
37:         merge_request_cleanup_schedule.mark_as_failed!
38:       end
39: 
40:       log_extra_metadata_on_done(:message, result[:message])
41:     end
42: 
43:     log_extra_metadata_on_done(:status, merge_request_cleanup_schedule.status)
44:   end
45: 
46:   def remaining_work_count
47:     MergeRequest::CleanupSchedule
48:       .scheduled_and_unstarted
49:       .limit(max_running_jobs)
50:       .count
51:   end
52: 
53:   def max_running_jobs
54:     MAX_RUNNING_JOBS
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def merge_request
60:     strong_memoize(:merge_request) do
61:       merge_request_cleanup_schedule&.merge_request
62:     end
63:   end
64: 
65:   def merge_request_cleanup_schedule
66:     strong_memoize(:merge_request_cleanup_schedule) do
67:       MergeRequest::CleanupSchedule.start_next
68:     end
69:   end
70: end

(End of file - total 70 lines)

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class RefreshService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
6: 
7:     attr_reader :push
8: 
9:     # Staggered delays for auto-merge worker enqueue.
10:     # Each MR gets a delay of index * INTERVAL seconds, spaced 3 seconds apart
11:     # (0s, 3s, 6s, ..., 57s) to avoid workers from the same or overlapping push
12:     # events landing at the same time. Capped at 20 MRs per push event; remaining
13:     # MRs are picked up in subsequent rounds as merges trigger new pushes.
14:     AUTO_MERGE_DELAY_INTERVAL = 3
15:     AUTO_MERGE_BATCH_LIMIT = 20
16: 
17:     def execute(oldrev, newrev, ref)
18:       @push = Gitlab::Git::Push.new(@project, oldrev, newrev, ref)
19:       return true unless @push.branch_push?
20: 
21:       refresh_merge_requests!
22:     end
23: 
24:     private
25: 
26:     def refresh_merge_requests!
27:       # n + 1: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-foss/issues/60289
28:       Gitlab::GitalyClient.allow_n_plus_1_calls { find_new_commits }
29:       # Be sure to close outstanding MRs before reloading them to avoid generating an
30:       # empty diff during a manual merge
31:       close_upon_missing_source_branch_ref
32: 
33:       post_merge_manually_merged
34: 
35:       link_forks_lfs_objects
36: 
37:       reload_merge_requests
38: 
39:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.each do |mr|
40:         outdate_suggestions(mr)
41:         abort_auto_merges(mr)
42:         mark_pending_todos_done(mr)
43:       end
44: 
45:       abort_ff_merge_requests_with_auto_merges
46: 
47:       cache_merge_requests_closing_issues
48: 
49:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.each do |mr|
50:         # Leave a system note if a branch was deleted/added
51:         comment_mr_branch_presence_changed(mr) if branch_added_or_removed?
52: 
53:         notify_about_push(mr)
54: 
55:         mark_mr_as_draft_from_commits(mr)
56:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: mr.author, merge_request: mr)
57:       end
58: 
59:       execute_async_workers
60: 
61:       true
62:     end
63: 
64:     def branch_added_or_removed?
65:       strong_memoize(:branch_added_or_removed) do
66:         @push.branch_added? || @push.branch_removed?
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def close_upon_missing_source_branch_ref
71:       # MergeRequest#reload_diff ignores not opened MRs. This means it won't
72:       # create an `empty` diff for `closed` MRs without a source branch, keeping
73:       # the latest diff state as the last _valid_ one.
74:       merge_requests_for_source_branch.reject(&:source_branch_exists?).each do |mr|
75:         Gitlab::AppLogger.info(
76:           message: 'Closing merge request due to missing source branch',
77:           merge_request_id: mr.id,
78:           merge_request_iid: mr.iid,
79:           project_id: mr.project_id,
80:           source_branch: mr.source_branch
81:         )
82: 
83:         MergeRequests::CloseService
84:           .new(project: mr.target_project, current_user: @current_user)
85:           .execute(mr)
86:       end
87:     end
88: 
89:     # Collect open merge requests that target same branch we push into
90:     # and close if push to master include last commit from merge request
91:     # We need this to close(as merged) merge requests that were merged into
92:     # target branch manually
93:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
94:     def post_merge_manually_merged
95:       commit_ids = @commits.map(&:id)
96:       merge_requests = @project.merge_requests.opened
97:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
98:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
99:         .preload_latest_diff_commit(@project)
100:         .where(target_branch: @push.branch_name).to_a
101:         .select(&:diff_head_commit)
102:         .select do |merge_request|
103:           commit_ids.include?(merge_request.diff_head_sha) &&
104:             merge_request.merge_request_diff.state != 'empty'
105:         end
106:       merge_requests = filter_merge_requests(merge_requests)
107: 
108:       return if merge_requests.empty?
109: 
110:       analyzer = Gitlab::BranchPushMergeCommitAnalyzer.new(
111:         @commits.reverse,
112:         relevant_commit_ids: merge_requests.map(&:diff_head_sha)
113:       )
114: 
115:       merge_requests.each do |merge_request|
116:         sha = analyzer.get_merge_commit(merge_request.diff_head_sha)
117:         merge_request.merge_commit_sha = sha
118:         merge_request.merged_commit_sha = sha
119: 
120:         # Look for a merged MR that includes the SHA to associate it with
121:         # the MR we're about to mark as merged.
122:         # Only the merged MRs without the event source would be considered
123:         # to avoid associating it with other MRs that we may have marked as merged here.
124:         source_merge_request = MergeRequestsFinder.new(
125:           @current_user,
126:           project_id: @project.id,
127:           merged_without_event_source: true,
128:           state: 'merged',
129:           sort: 'merged_at',
130:           commit_sha: sha
131:         ).execute.first
132: 
133:         source = source_merge_request || @project.commit(sha)
134: 
135:         MergeRequests::PostMergeService
136:           .new(project: merge_request.target_project, current_user: @current_user)
137:           .execute(merge_request, source)
138:       end
139:     end
140:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
141: 
142:     # Link LFS objects that exists in forks but does not exists in merge requests
143:     # target project
144:     def link_forks_lfs_objects
145:       return unless @push.branch_updated?
146: 
147:       merge_requests_for_forks.find_each do |mr|
148:         LinkLfsObjectsService
149:           .new(project: mr.target_project)
150:           .execute(mr, oldrev: @push.oldrev, newrev: @push.newrev)
151:       end
152:     end
153: 
154:     # Refresh merge request diff if we push to source or target branch of merge request
155:     # Note: we should update merge requests from forks too
156:     def reload_merge_requests
157:       merge_requests = @project.merge_requests.opened
158:         .by_source_or_target_branch(@push.branch_name)
159:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
160:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
161: 
162:       merge_requests_from_forks = merge_requests_for_forks
163:         .preload_project_and_latest_diff
164:         .preload_merge_data(@project)
165: 
166:       merge_requests_array = merge_requests.to_a + merge_requests_from_forks.to_a
167:       filtered_merge_requests = filter_merge_requests(merge_requests_array)
168: 
169:       recheck_merge_requests_batched(filtered_merge_requests)
170: 
171:       # Upcoming method calls need the refreshed version of
172:       # @source_merge_requests diffs (for MergeRequest#commit_shas for instance).
173:       merge_requests_for_source_branch(reload: true)
174:     end
175: 
176:     def recheck_merge_requests_batched(filtered_merge_requests)
177:       source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs = []
178:       all_mr_ids = []
179: 
180:       filtered_merge_requests.each do |merge_request|
181:         all_mr_ids << merge_request.id
182: 
183:         if branch_and_project_match?(merge_request) || @push.force_push?
184:           merge_request.reload_diff(current_user)
185:           schedule_duo_code_review(merge_request)
186:           source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs << merge_request
187:         elsif merge_request.merge_request_diff.includes_any_commits?(push_commit_ids)
188:           merge_request.reload_diff(current_user)
189:         end
190:       end
191: 
192:       MergeRequest.batch_mark_as_unchecked(all_mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs)
193: 
194:       # Clear existing merge error if the push were directed at the
195:       # source branch. Clearing the error when the target branch
196:       # changes will hide the error from the user.
197:       MergeRequest.batch_clear_merge_error(source_branch_or_force_pushed_mrs.map(&:id))
198: 
199:       enqueue_auto_merge_for_unchecked(filtered_merge_requests)
200:     end

(Showing lines 1-200 of 421. Use offset=201 to continue.)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CloseService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include RemovesRefs
6: 
7:     def execute(merge_request, commit = nil, skip_reset: false)
8:       return merge_request unless authorized_to_close?(merge_request)
9: 
10:       use_primary(merge_request, skip_reset) do |merge_request|
11:         # If we close MergeRequest we want to ignore validation
12:         # so we can close broken one (Ex. fork project removed)
13:         merge_request.allow_broken = true
14: 
15:         if merge_request.close
16:           expire_unapproved_key(merge_request)
17:           create_event(merge_request)
18:           merge_request_activity_counter.track_close_mr_action(user: current_user)
19:           create_note(merge_request)
20:           notification_service.async.close_mr(merge_request, current_user)
21:           todo_service.close_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
22:           execute_hooks(merge_request, 'close')
23:           invalidate_all_users_cache_count(merge_request)
24:           merge_request.invalidate_project_counter_caches
25:           cleanup_environments(merge_request)
26:           deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
27:           abort_auto_merge(merge_request, 'merge request was closed')
28:           cleanup_refs(merge_request)
29:           trigger_merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
30:         end
31: 
32:         merge_request
33:       end
34:     end
35: 
36:     private
37: 
38:     def use_primary(merge_request, skip_reset)
39:       return yield(merge_request) if skip_reset
40: 
41:       ::Gitlab::Database::LoadBalancing::SessionMap.current(merge_request.load_balancer).use_primary do
42:         yield(merge_request.reset)
43:       end
44:     end
45: 
46:     def create_event(merge_request)
47:       # Making sure MergeRequest::Metrics updates are in sync with
48:       # Event creation.
49:       Event.transaction do
50:         close_event = event_service.close_mr(merge_request, current_user)
51:         merge_request_metrics_service(merge_request).close(close_event)
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     def expire_unapproved_key(merge_request)
56:       nil
57:     end
58: 
59:     def trigger_merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
60:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
61:     end
62: 
63:     def authorized_to_close?(merge_request)
64:       params[:skip_authorization].present? || can?(current_user, :update_merge_request, merge_request)
65:     end
66:   end
67: end
68: 
69: MergeRequests::CloseService.prepend_mod

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class MergeData < ApplicationRecord
5:     include PartitionedTable
6:     include ShaAttribute
7:     include Transitionable
8: 
9:     self.table_name = 'merge_requests_merge_data'
10:     self.primary_key = 'merge_request_id'
11: 
12:     partitioned_by :merge_request_id, strategy: :int_range, partition_size: 10_000_000,
13:       sequence_name: 'merge_requests_id_seq'
14: 
15:     MERGE_STATUSES = {
16:       unchecked: 0,
17:       preparing: 1,
18:       checking: 2,
19:       can_be_merged: 3,
20:       cannot_be_merged: 4,
21:       cannot_be_merged_recheck: 5,
22:       cannot_be_merged_rechecking: 6
23:     }.freeze
24: 
25:     enum :merge_status, MERGE_STATUSES, scope: false, instance_methods: false
26: 
27:     belongs_to :merge_request, inverse_of: :merge_data
28:     belongs_to :project
29:     belongs_to :merge_user, class_name: 'User'
30: 
31:     validates :project, presence: true
32:     validates :merge_request, presence: true
33:     validates :merge_status, presence: true
34: 
35:     sha_attribute :merge_commit_sha
36:     sha_attribute :merged_commit_sha
37:     sha_attribute :merge_ref_sha
38:     sha_attribute :squash_commit_sha
39:     sha_attribute :in_progress_merge_commit_sha
40: 
41:     serialize :merge_params, type: Hash # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordSerialize -- Extraction to a new table
42: 
43:     state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
44:       event :mark_as_preparing do
45:         transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
46:       end
47: 
48:       event :mark_as_unchecked do
49:         transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
50:         transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
51:       end
52: 
53:       event :mark_as_checking do
54:         transition unchecked: :checking
55:         transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
56:       end
57: 
58:       event :mark_as_mergeable do
59:         transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
60:       end
61: 
62:       event :mark_as_unmergeable do
63:         transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
64:       end
65: 
66:       state :preparing
67:       state :unchecked
68:       state :cannot_be_merged_recheck
69:       state :checking
70:       state :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
71:       state :can_be_merged
72:       state :cannot_be_merged
73: 
74:       around_transition do |merge_data, _transition, block|
75:         Gitlab::Timeless.timeless(merge_data, &block)
76:       end
77: 
78:       # TODO: Move before_transition and after_transition callbacks from MergeRequest
79:       #   when MergeRequest no longer handles merge_status transitions:
80: 
81:       def check_state?(merge_status)
82:         [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
83:       end
84:     end
85: 
86:     # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
87:     # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
88:     def public_merge_status
89:       cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
90:     end
91:   end
92: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CleanupRefsService
5:     include BaseServiceUtility
6: 
7:     TIME_THRESHOLD = 14.days
8: 
9:     attr_reader :merge_request
10: 
11:     def self.schedule(merge_request)
12:       merge_request.create_cleanup_schedule(scheduled_at: TIME_THRESHOLD.from_now)
13:     end
14: 
15:     def initialize(merge_request)
16:       @merge_request = merge_request
17:       @repository = merge_request.project.repository
18:       @ref_path = merge_request.ref_path
19:       @ref_head_sha = @repository.commit(merge_request.ref_path)&.id
20:       @merge_ref_sha = merge_request.merge_ref_head&.id
21:     end
22: 
23:     def execute
24:       return error("Merge request is not scheduled to be cleaned up yet.") unless scheduled?
25:       return error("Merge request has not been closed nor merged for #{TIME_THRESHOLD.inspect}.") unless eligible?
26: 
27:       return error('Failed to cache merge ref sha.') unless cache_merge_ref_sha
28: 
29:       delete_refs if repository.exists?
30: 
31:       return error('Failed to update schedule.') unless update_schedule
32: 
33:       success
34:     rescue Gitlab::Git::Repository::GitError, Gitlab::Git::CommandError => e
35:       error(e.message)
36:     end
37: 
38:     private
39: 
40:     attr_reader :repository, :ref_path, :ref_head_sha, :merge_ref_sha
41: 
42:     def scheduled?
43:       merge_request.cleanup_schedule.present? && merge_request.cleanup_schedule.scheduled_at <= Time.current
44:     end
45: 
46:     def eligible?
47:       return met_time_threshold?(merge_request.metrics&.latest_closed_at) if merge_request.closed?
48: 
49:       merge_request.merged? && met_time_threshold?(merge_request.metrics&.merged_at)
50:     end
51: 
52:     def met_time_threshold?(attr)
53:       attr.nil? || attr.to_i <= TIME_THRESHOLD.ago.to_i
54:     end
55: 
56:     def cache_merge_ref_sha
57:       return true if merge_ref_sha.nil?
58: 
59:       # Caching the merge ref sha is needed before we delete the merge ref so
60:       # we can still show the merge ref diff (via `MergeRequest#merge_ref_head`)
61:       merge_request.update_merge_ref_sha(merge_ref_sha)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def delete_refs
65:       merge_request.schedule_cleanup_refs
66:     end
67: 
68:     def update_schedule
69:       merge_request.cleanup_schedule.update(completed_at: Time.current)
70:     end
71:   end
72: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   # MergeService class
5:   #
6:   # Do git merge and in case of success
7:   # mark merge request as merged and execute all hooks and notifications
8:   # Executed when you do merge via GitLab UI
9:   #
10:   class MergeService < MergeRequests::MergeBaseService
11:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
12: 
13:     GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'An error occurred while merging'
14: 
15:     delegate :merge_jid, :state, to: :@merge_request
16: 
17:     def execute(merge_request, options = {})
18:       return if merge_request.merged?
19:       return unless exclusive_lease(merge_request).try_obtain
20: 
21:       merge_strategy_class = options[:merge_strategy] || MergeRequests::MergeStrategies::FromSourceBranch
22:       @merge_strategy = merge_strategy_class.new(merge_request, current_user, merge_params: params, options: options)
23: 
24:       @merge_request = merge_request
25:       @options = options
26: 
27:       # Lease may already be held by another merge with the same source branch.
28:       # This is safe - the lease protects downstream MRs (via target_branch_pending_deletion_check!),
29:       # and that protection is already in place from the other process.
30:       obtain_branch_deletion_lease if delete_source_branch?
31:       jid = merge_jid
32: 
33:       validate!
34: 
35:       merge_request.in_locked_state do
36:         if commit
37:           after_merge
38:           success
39:         end
40:       end
41: 
42:       log_info("Merge process finished on JID #{jid} with state #{state}")
43:     rescue MergeError, MergeRequests::MergeStrategies::StrategyError => e
44:       handle_merge_error(log_message: e.message, save_message_on_model: true)
45:     ensure
46:       release_branch_deletion_lease unless merge_request.merged?
47:       exclusive_lease(merge_request).cancel
48:     end
49: 
50:     private
51: 
52:     def validate!
53:       authorization_check!
54:       error_check!
55:       target_branch_pending_deletion_check!
56:       validate_strategy!
57:       updated_check!
58:     end
59: 
60:     def authorization_check!
61:       unless @merge_request.can_be_merged_by?(current_user)
62:         raise_error('You are not allowed to merge this merge request')
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     def validate_strategy!
67:       @merge_strategy.validate!
68:     end
69: 
70:     def target_branch_pending_deletion_check!
71:       lease_key = MergeRequests::DeleteSourceBranchWorker.lease_key(@merge_request.target_project_id, @merge_request.target_branch)
72:       return unless Gitlab::ExclusiveLease.get_uuid(lease_key)
73: 
74:       raise_error(_('The target branch is scheduled for deletion. Please wait for the branch to be retargeted.'))
75:     end
76: 
77:     def updated_check!
78:       unless source_matches?
79:         raise_error('Branch has been updated since the merge was requested. '\
80:                     'Please review the changes.')
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def commit
85:       log_info("Git merge started on JID #{merge_jid}")
86: 
87:       merge_result = try_merge { @merge_strategy.execute_git_merge! }
88: 
89:       commit_sha = merge_result[:commit_sha]
90:       raise_error(GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE) unless commit_sha
91: 
92:       log_info("Git merge finished on JID #{merge_jid} commit #{commit_sha}")
93: 
94:       new_merge_request_attributes = {
95:         merged_commit_sha: commit_sha,
96:         merge_commit_sha: merge_result[:merge_commit_sha],
97:         squash_commit_sha: merge_result[:squash_commit_sha]
98:       }.compact
99: 
100:       merge_request.update!(new_merge_request_attributes) if new_merge_request_attributes.present?
101: 
102:       commit_sha
103:     ensure
104:       merge_request.update_and_mark_in_progress_merge_commit_sha(nil)
105:       log_info("Merge request marked in progress")
106:     end
107: 
108:     def try_merge
109:       yield
110:     rescue Gitlab::Git::PreReceiveError => e
111:       raise MergeError, "Something went wrong during merge pre-receive hook. #{e.message}".strip
112:     rescue StandardError => e
113:       handle_merge_error(log_message: e.message)
114:       raise_error(GENERIC_ERROR_MESSAGE)
115:     end
116: 
117:     def after_merge
118:       # Need to store `merge_jid` in a variable since `MergeRequests::PostMergeService`
119:       # will call `MergeRequest#mark_as_merged` and will unset `merge_jid`.
120:       jid = merge_jid

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   # PostMergeService class
5:   #
6:   # Mark existing merge request as merged
7:   # and execute all hooks and notifications
8:   #
9:   class PostMergeService < MergeRequests::BaseService
10:     include RemovesRefs
11: 
12:     MAX_RETARGET_MERGE_REQUESTS = 4
13:     BATCH_SIZE = 100
14: 
15:     def execute(merge_request, source = nil)
16:       return if merge_request.merged?
17: 
18:       # Mark the merge request as merged, everything that happens afterwards is
19:       # executed once
20:       unless merge_request.mark_as_merged
21:         Gitlab::AppLogger.warn(
22:           message: 'Failed to mark merge request as merged',
23:           merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
24:           merge_request_iid: merge_request.iid,
25:           project_id: merge_request.project_id,
26:           delete_source_branch: params[:delete_source_branch],
27:           errors: merge_request.errors.full_messages
28:         )
29:       end
30: 
31:       create_event(merge_request)
32:       todo_service.merge_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
33: 
34:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_merge_mr_action(user: current_user)
35: 
36:       create_note(merge_request, source)
37:       close_issues(merge_request)
38:       notification_service.merge_mr(merge_request, current_user)
39:       invalidate_all_users_cache_count(merge_request)
40:       merge_request.invalidate_project_counter_caches
41:       delete_non_latest_diffs(merge_request)
42:       cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
43:       cleanup_environments(merge_request)
44:       cleanup_refs(merge_request)
45:       deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
46:       cancel_auto_merges_targeting_source_branch(merge_request)
47:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
48: 
49:       execute_hooks(merge_request, 'merge')
50:     end
51: 
52:     def create_note(merge_request, source)
53:       SystemNoteService.change_status(
54:         merge_request,
55:         merge_request.target_project,
56:         current_user,
57:         merge_request.state,
58:         source
59:       )
60:     end
61: 
62:     private
63: 
64:     def close_issues(merge_request)
65:       return unless merge_request.target_branch == project.default_branch
66: 
67:       # We need to do this here to include closing messages from merge and squash commit
68:       merge_request.cache_closing_issues_after_merge!(current_user)
69: 
70:       if merge_request.target_project.has_external_issue_tracker?
71:         merge_request.closes_issues(current_user).each do |issue|
72:           close_issue(issue, merge_request)
73:         end
74:       else
75:         delay = 0
76:         merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.preload_issue
77:                      .find_each(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE).with_index(1) do |closing_issue, index| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Would require exact redefinition of the method
78:           delay += 5 if (index % 5) == 0
79:           close_issue(closing_issue.issue, merge_request, !closing_issue.from_mr_description, delay: delay)
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def close_issue(issue, merge_request, skip_authorization = false, delay: 0)
85:       # We are intentionally only closing Issues asynchronously (excluding ExternalIssues)
86:       # as the worker only supports finding an Issue. We are also only experiencing
87:       # SQL timeouts when closing an Issue.
88:       if issue.is_a?(Issue)
89:         # Doing this check here only to save a scheduled worker. The worker will also do this policy check.
90:         return if !skip_authorization && !current_user.can?(:update_issue, issue)
91:         return unless issue.autoclose_by_merged_closing_merge_request?
92: 
93:         worker_args = [project.id,
94:           current_user.id,
95:           issue.id,
96:           merge_request.id,
97:           { skip_authorization: skip_authorization }]
98:         MergeRequests::CloseIssueWorker.perform_in(delay.minutes, *worker_args)
99:       else
100:         Issues::CloseService.new(container: project, current_user: current_user).execute(issue, commit: merge_request)
101:       end
102:     end
103: 
104:     def delete_non_latest_diffs(merge_request)
105:       DeleteNonLatestDiffsService.new(merge_request).execute
106:     end
107: 
108:     def create_merge_event(merge_request, current_user)
109:       EventCreateService.new.merge_mr(merge_request, current_user)
110:     end
111: 
112:     def create_event(merge_request)
113:       # Making sure MergeRequest::Metrics updates are in sync with
114:       # Event creation.
115:       Event.transaction do
116:         merge_event = create_merge_event(merge_request, current_user)
117:         merge_request_metrics_service(merge_request).merge(merge_event)
118:       end
119:     end
120:

todos.content: Step 1: Orient in the codebase (project layout, central model, shared pieces)
Step 2: Map the MergeRequest contract and included shared behavior
Step 3: Trace MR create/update lifecycle and fan-out effects
Step 4: Exhaustive audit of MergeRequest dependents by area
Step 5: Trace visibility/permission guards around MRs
Step 6: Assess blast radius and rank risk by dependent
Step 7: Produce change + verification map (models, dependents, specs)
Final: JSON summary of actions and findings

content.text:
Here is the complete audit, working through each step in order.

---

## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

This is the GitLab Rails monolith (CE + EE code paths; this checkout appears to be the FOSS tree). The code is organized by the standard Rails layout plus large domain namespaces under `app/`, `lib/gitlab/`, `lib/api/`, and `ee/` (when present). The central model for a proposed code change is:

- `app/models/merge_request.rb` — the `MergeRequest` ActiveRecord model.

It is built on a stack of shared concerns in `app/models/concerns/` (e.g., `Issuable`, `Noteable`, `Referable`, `Awards`, `TimeTrackable`), on domain utilities in `lib/gitlab/merge_requests/`, and on a family of child/related models in:

- `app/models/merge_request/` (metrics, cleanup schedule, diff metadata, etc.)
- `app/models/merge_requests/` (merge data, merge schedule, generated refs, linked work items, versioned MR)
- `app/models/merge_request_diff.rb`, `app/models/merge_request_diff_*.rb`, `app/models/merge_request_assignee.rb`, `app/models/merge_request_reviewer.rb`, etc.

The service layer lives in `app/services/merge_requests/`, workers in `app/workers/` and `app/workers/merge_requests/`, REST API in `lib/api/merge_requests.rb`, GraphQL in `app/graphql/types/merge_request_type.rb` and `app/graphql/mutations/merge_requests/`, serializers in `app/serializers/`, and finders in `app/finders/merge_requests_finder.rb`.

- Several merge-related columns (`merge_params`, `merge_status`, `merge_commit_sha`, `merge_user_id`, etc.) are currently **dual-written** to the new `merge_requests_merge_data` table behind the `merge_requests_merge_data_dual_write` feature flag: see `with_merge_data_sync`/`save_merge_data_changes` at `app/models/merge_request.rb:2861-3120`.
- It defines scopes, reference patterns (`!123`), mergeability checks, diff helpers, CI variable helpers, and async methods (`merge_async`, `rebase_async`, `schedule_cleanup_refs`).

## Step 3 — Trace how a merge request is created and updated

### Creation path

1. **Entry points**
   - Web: `app/controllers/projects/merge_requests_controller.rb:3` → `MergeRequests::CreateService`.
   - API: `lib/api/merge_requests.rb:444` (`authorize! :create_merge_request_from, :create_merge_request_in`) → `MergeRequests::CreateService`.
   - Issue “Create MR”: `app/controllers/projects/issues_controller.rb:245` / `app/services/issues/update_service.rb:176` → `MergeRequests::CreateFromIssueService`.
   - Email: `lib/gitlab/email/handler/create_merge_request_handler.rb:12`.

2. **`MergeRequests::CreateService#execute`** (`app/services/merge_requests/create_service.rb:5`)
   - Builds the MR, sets source/target projects, deletes `merge_after` param.
   - `before_create` checks branch existence and skips diff creation via `skip_ensure_merge_request_diff = true` (line 54).
   - Calls `create(merge_request)` (inherits `IssuableBaseService`).
   - `after_create` (line 23): enqueues `NewMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(issuable.id, current_user_id)` **asynchronously**, then marks the MR as `preparing`.

3. **`NewMergeRequestWorker`** (`app/workers/new_merge_request_worker.rb:19`)
   - Runs `MergeRequests::AfterCreateService.new(...).execute(issuable)` if the MR is not already prepared.

4. **`MergeRequests::AfterCreateService`** (`app/services/merge_requests/after_create_service.rb:5`)
   - Inline: `ensure_merge_request_diff` (creates/persists the first diff).
   - Creates a head pipeline (`create_pipeline_for` sync or async depending on feature flag).
   - `check_mergeability` marks unchecked and triggers async mergeability check.
   - Records open event, sends notifications (`notification_service.new_merge_request`), creates todos (`todo_service.new_merge_request`), caches closing issues, writes diff cache, creates cross-references, links LFS objects, executes hooks/integrations, tracks internal events.

### Update path

1. **Entry points**
   - Web: `app/controllers/projects/merge_requests_controller.rb` → `MergeRequests::UpdateService`.
   - API: `lib/api/merge_requests.rb:838` → `find_merge_request_with_access(..., :update_merge_request)` → update service.
   - GraphQL: `app/graphql/mutations/merge_requests/update.rb` → `MergeRequests::UpdateService`.

2. **`MergeRequests::UpdateService#execute`** (`app/services/merge_requests/update_service.rb:14`)
   - Handles draft title conversion, merge schedule changes.
   - Delegates to specialized services for single-attribute updates (`assignee_ids`, `spend_time`) or falls back to `general_fallback` → `update(merge_request)` (via `IssuableBaseService`).
   - `handle_changes` (line 27): compares old/new labels, assignees, reviewers, mentions; resolves todos; fires notifications; handles target/source branch changes.
   - On branch change (line 60): destroys `merge_head_diff`, marks `unchecked`, triggers async mergeability check.
   - `after_update` (line 76): re-caches closing issues and triggers work-item update subscriptions.

### Push-driven refresh path

- `UpdateMergeRequestsWorker` (`app/workers/update_merge_requests_worker.rb:28`) **async** → `MergeRequests::RefreshService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/refresh_service.rb:17`).
- Refresh service:
  - Closes MRs whose source branch is gone (`MergeRequests::CloseService`).
  - Detects manual merges and runs `PostMergeService`.
  - Reloads diffs, aborts auto-merges, marks todos done, caches closing issues, adds system notes, notifies about push, triggers async web-hooks worker.

### Merge path

- Web/API/Quick action → `MergeRequests::MergeOrchestrationService` or directly `MergeRequests::MergeService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/merge_service.rb:17`).
- `MergeService`: obtains exclusive lease, validates permissions/branch state, runs git merge via strategy, updates `merged_commit_sha`/`merge_commit_sha`/`squash_commit_sha`.
- Then `after_merge` → `MergeRequests::PostMergeService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/post_merge_service.rb:15`):
  - Marks merged, creates merge event + metrics, todos, system note, closes linked issues (async `CloseIssueWorker`), notifications, invalidates caches, deletes non-latest diffs, cancels review apps, cleans up environments, cleans up refs, deactivates Pages deployments, executes hooks.

### Close path

- `MergeRequests::CloseService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/close_service.rb:7`):
  - Checks `update_merge_request` permission, sets `allow_broken`, calls `merge_request.close`.
  - Creates close event + metrics, system note, notifications, todos, hooks, invalidates caches, cleans up environments, deactivates Pages, aborts auto-merge, cleans up refs, triggers GraphQL status update.

### What runs inline vs async

- **Inline in request**: validation, persistence, basic attribute updates, some permission checks, GraphQL subscription triggers via `run_after_commit`.
- **Async workers**: `NewMergeRequestWorker`, `UpdateMergeRequestsWorker`, `MergeWorker`, `RebaseWorker`, `AutoMergeProcessWorker`, `MergeRequests::CloseIssueWorker`, `MergeRequests::Refresh::NotifyAboutPushWorker`, `MergeRequests::Refresh::WebHooksWorker`, `MergeRequestCleanupRefsWorker`, `KeepAroundRefsWorker`, `UpdateHeadPipelineForMergeRequestWorker`, `MergeRequestMergeabilityCheckWorker`, etc.

## Step 5 — Visibility and permission guards

Policy definitions:

- `app/policies/project_policy.rb:516-539` — prevents all MR abilities when merge requests or repository are disabled.
- `app/policies/project_policy.rb:745` — allows push-to-MR when the project is public/internal and the MR allows pushes.
- `app/policies/merge_request_policy.rb:7-52` — MR-specific rules: prevent reopen on merged, gate `accept_merge_request` behind `read_merge_request`, enable `approve_merge_request`, gate `set_merge_request_metadata` behind `admin_merge_request`.
- `app/policies/issuable_policy.rb:43-45` — author/assignee can update/reopen MR if they can read it.
- `app/policies/merge_requests_closing_issues_policy.rb:8-11` — closing-issue read requires `read_merge_request`.

Enforcement points:

- Controllers: `app/controllers/projects/merge_requests/application_controller.rb:6` (`authorize_read_merge_request!`), line 59 re-checks `can? :read_merge_request, @issuable`.
- Controller create: `app/controllers/projects/merge_requests/creations_controller.rb:102` checks `create_merge_request_in`.
- API helper: `lib/api/helpers.rb:383` `find_merge_request_with_access(iid, access_level = :read_merge_request)`.
- REST endpoints: `lib/api/merge_requests.rb:401` (`read_merge_request`), `:444` (`create_merge_request`), `:480` (`delete_merge_request`), `:838` (`update_merge_request`), plus fine-grained permissions like `read_merge_request_diff`, `read_merge_request_commit`, etc.
- GraphQL type: `app/graphql/types/merge_request_type.rb:13` `authorize :read_merge_request`.
- GraphQL mutations: `app/graphql/mutations/merge_requests/base.rb:21` `authorize :update_merge_request`; `create.rb:47` `authorize :create_merge_request_from`.
- Services: `MergeRequests::CreateService` lines 71-72 (`create_merge_request_from`/`_in`), `CloseService` line 64 (`update_merge_request`), `UpdateReviewerStateService` line 6, `RequestReviewService` line 6, `AddContextService` line 6, `BuildService` lines 102/109/113.
- Model-level participant filter: `app/models/merge_request.rb:1116` `Ability.allowed?(participant, :read_merge_request, self)`.
- Global readable filter: `app/models/ability.rb:67` `merge_requests.select { |mr| allowed?(user, :read_merge_request, mr) }`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of a contract change

Risk ranking assumes the rework changes how the MR stores and tears down attached data.

### Critical / most likely to break

| Dependent | Why it is high risk |
|---|---|
| `app/models/merge_request.rb` | The contract itself; every callback, association, state machine, dual-write hook, and teardown method lives here. |
| `app/models/merge_request_diff.rb` and `app/models/merge_request_diff_*.rb` | Diffs are created in `after_create`, reloaded on branch change, and partially deleted after merge. Any change to MR lifecycle impacts diff existence. |
| `app/models/merge_request/metrics.rb` | Lifecycle events (open/close/merge) write metrics; teardown must keep metrics consistent. |
| `app/models/merge_requests/merge_data.rb` | New dual-write table. A contract change around attached data may move more state here or require cleanup. |
| `app/models/note.rb`, `app/models/diff_note.rb`, `app/models/discussion_note.rb`, `app/models/legacy_diff_note.rb` | Notes/discussions are polymorphic on MR. Teardown must not orphan resolvable diff notes. |
| `app/models/todo.rb`, `app/models/award_emoji.rb`, `app/models/timelog.rb`, `app/models/label.rb` (label_links) | Issuable/Noteable/TimeTrackable/Awardable behaviour cascades from MR. |
| `app/models/event.rb`, `app/models/resource_state_event.rb`, `app/models/resource_label_event.rb`, `app/models/resource_milestone_event.rb`, `app/models/resource_events/merge_request_assignment_event.rb` | Event/resource-event records reference MR type. |
| `app/models/merge_request_assignee.rb`, `app/models/merge_request_reviewer.rb`, `app/models/merge_request_user_mention.rb`, `app/models/review.rb`, `app/models/draft_note.rb` | Direct attached records; must be created/destroyed consistently. |
| `app/models/merge_requests_closing_issues.rb`, `app/models/deployment_merge_request.rb`, `app/models/merge_requests/linked_work_item.rb` | Relationship join tables. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/create_service.rb`, `update_service.rb`, `close_service.rb`, `post_merge_service.rb`, `merge_service.rb` | Core lifecycle services that create and tear down attached data. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/refresh_service.rb`, `reload_diffs_service.rb`, `cleanup_refs_service.rb`, `delete_non_latest_diffs_service.rb`, `retarget_chain_service.rb` | Push/merge/close side effects. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/after_create_service.rb` | Creates diff, pipeline, notes, todos, hooks right after MR creation. |
| `app/workers/merge_worker.rb`, `rebase_worker.rb`, `new_merge_request_worker.rb`, `update_merge_requests_worker.rb`, `merge_request_cleanup_refs_worker.rb`, `auto_merge_process_worker.rb` | Async lifecycle workers that may run while contract is in flux. |

### High

| Dependent | Why |
|---|---|
| `app/services/merge_requests/mergeability/*_service.rb`, `mergeability_check_service.rb`, `mergeability_check_batch_service.rb` | Merge status is part of the contract; dual-written to `merge_data`. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/approval_service.rb`, `remove_approval_service.rb`, `execute_approval_hooks_service.rb` | Approval state stored on MR/approvable concern. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/rebase_service.rb`, `squash_service.rb`, `merge_to_ref_service.rb`, `merge_orchestration_service.rb` | Git state and ref management. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/conflicts/*_service.rb` | Conflict state depends on latest diff. |
| `app/services/merge_requests/build_service.rb`, `create_from_issue_service.rb`, `push_options_handler_service.rb` | Alternate creation paths. |
| `app/models/ci/pipeline.rb`, `app/services/ci/*` | Pipelines attach to MR; teardown must handle `head_pipeline_id`, `pipelines_for_merge_request`. |
| `app/services/notes/*_service.rb`, `draft_notes/*_service.rb` | Notes/drafts are MR children. |
| `app/serializers/merge_request_*.rb`, `app/graphql/types/merge_request_type.rb` | Expose MR fields and associations; fields may disappear or move. |

### Medium

- Analytics: `lib/gitlab/analytics/cycle_analytics/*`, `app/models/analytics/cycle_analytics/merge_request_stage_event.rb`
- Search/recent items: `lib/gitlab/search/recent_merge_requests.rb`, `app/graphql/resolvers/users/recently_viewed_*_resolver.rb`
- Usage counters: `lib/gitlab/usage_data_counters/merge_request_activity_unique_counter.rb`, `merge_request_widget_extension_counter.rb`
- Import/export: `lib/gitlab/import_export/*`, `app/workers/projects/import_export/after_import_merge_requests_worker.rb`
- Webhooks/integrations: `lib/gitlab/hook_data/merge_request_builder.rb`, `app/services/merge_requests/refresh/web_hooks_service.rb`
- Background migrations: `lib/gitlab/background_migration/*merge_request*`

### What must be re-verified

- MR create/update/close/merge flows end-to-end.
- Diff creation, reloading, and deletion.
- Note/discussion/draft note lifecycle.
- Approval/assignee/reviewer state.
- Pipeline association and `head_pipeline_id`.
- Metrics/event/todo/label/milestone cleanup.
- Gitaly ref cleanup (`schedule_cleanup_refs`, `CleanupRefsService`).
- API/GraphQL responses still include expected fields.
- Import/export round-trips.
- Loose-FK cleanup for `MergeRequestDiffCommit`.
